selected_important_turn.text: Home cooking's the best, yumyums! We're planning on doing a lot of fishing since my son's been in love with fishing toys and is currently in love with eating fish (he hasn't gotten tired of eating it every night for dinner). We're thinking of cooking what we catch every night. Do you have any activities planned for your camping trip?
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn immediately preceded the opponent's concrete allocation proposal (their integrative-sounding offer). It was therefore a high-leverage moment to shape the framing of the upcoming deal — either locking in collaborative options or leaving ambiguity that allowed competitive cycling. Clearer signals here could have made the opponent's offer more stable and reduced later escalation.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: As written it stays at rapport-building and does not state the speaker's priorities or tradeoffs. You had the chance to make your top concerns explicit (e.g., water reliability for a child) or to propose a simple trade (we'll take extra firewood in exchange for extra water). That missing clarity left room for misunderstanding and for the opponent to later retract or harden positions.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Use this conversational opening to state a concise, relevant preference or trade-off (e.g., emphasize water is critical for your child and indicate what you could give up in exchange), or to invite a conditional offer ('If you can take X, we'd be willing to take Y'). The goal is to convert rapport into a concrete integrative signal that narrows options constructively before they propose a deal.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (moderate) — clarifying priorities enables mutually beneficial trades rather than zero-sum haggling.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (moderate) — transparent needs let splits be tailored to who values what.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (moderate to strong) — reduces ambiguity and the chance of retractions/resistance.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (small to moderate) — being explicit while cooperative builds trust and reduces frustration.

value2reason.Low: We plan to be catching fish while camping and want to eat that
value2reason.Medium: We plan to be cooking a lot of the fish that we catch at the lake and may need additional fuel to cook them
value2reason.High: The only fresh water source is the lake at which we are camping at and I do not trust water filtration devices

value2reason.Low: I would be able to warm up the water to keep my campsite clean, to heat water to bathe, to offer heated water to my guests to wash up, to keep my guests warm in the evening, to make s'mores
value2reason.Medium: To have extra food to share, to be prepared if  there are others who drop by and need it.
value2reason.High: To be able to stay clean, hydrated and cooks food I would need more water

dialogue.text: Hello🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Hi! I'm going camping soon with my family at a lake! We're looking forward to it so much since we haven't been able to get out due to Covid. Are you going camping too?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Yes! I am also going with my family. My husband and I are essential workers but his hours all got cut. I got laid off from my job for part of the time.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: oh man that sucks ☹️! I know Congress is currently debating all of the unemployment stuff too. Where do you plan to be going camping?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: We didn't get unemployment or a stimulus check. ☹️ We are planning a trip to Missouri, Lake of the Ozarks. Our younger children have been cooped up so long we are hoping to let them get some sun and swimming in!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: That sounds nice, maybe everyone just needs some fun to stay positive about everything. We're going camping along a lake and plan to do a lot of fishing. My husband got these filtration thingies so that we can drink the lake water but I don't really trust them. This will be my kid's first ever camping trip so we're super excited to make it fun for him.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: We have 7 people in our family,.. the youngest two kids have hardly been anywhere because of working. I think our camping area has those old fashioned hand pumps.☹️
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Your two youngest kids are working? How old is everyone? If there's a lot of people, it will be easier to split up the duties and get things done. My son's 3 years old so as much as he's eager to help, there's not much he can do lol.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ha Ha...no my too youngest have hardly been anywhere because of us adults working,  I have teens (17, 15), tweens (13) and littles (10, 7). We 3 boys and 3 girls. We have a 21 year old but he's in a band so h can't go. They all pitch in anytime. They eat a lot so they have to cook from scratch at home. Awww 3 is so adorable...that's when they are sure they can do and will work so hard!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Home cooking's the best, yumyums! We're planning on doing a lot of fishing since my son's been in love with fishing toys and is currently in love with eating fish (he hasn't gotten tired of eating it every night for dinner). We're thinking of cooking what we catch every night. Do you have any activities planned for your camping trip?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I really need to prioritize water since the lake is our only fresh water source and I don’t trust the filtration devices. If you’re okay with less firewood, we could split the water more evenly.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand the importance of water. Let’s split the 3 water packages evenly—1 each. For firewood, I’d be happy with 2 packages, and you can have 1. For food, I suggest we split them 2-1. That way, we both have enough to stay hydrated and prepared for guests.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m okay with 1 water package, but I need at least 2 firewood packages to cook the fish we’ll catch. If you take 1 water, I’ll give you 1 food in exchange for 1 firewood. Let’s make sure we both have enough for the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m okay with 1 water package, but I need at least 2 firewood packages to cook the fish we’ll catch. If you take 1 water, I’ll give you 1 food in exchange for 1 firewood. Let’s make sure we both have enough for the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 firewood packages to cook the fish we’ll catch. If you take 1 water, I’ll give you 1 food in exchange for 1 firewood. Let’s make sure we both have enough for the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m willing to take 1 water package and 2 firewood packages if you give me 2 food packages. That way, we both have enough for cooking and staying hydrated, and I can help keep the campsite clean with the firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 firewood packages to cook the fish we’ll catch. If you take 1 water, I’ll give you 1 food in exchange for 1 firewood. Let’s make sure we both have enough for the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m willing to accept 1 water, 2 firewood, and 2 food if you agree. This balances our needs, ensures we can cook and stay hydrated, and allows me to keep the campsite clean with the firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 firewood packages to cook the fish we’ll catch. If you take 1 water and 2 food, I’ll give you 1 firewood. Let’s finalize this split so we can prepare properly.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I agree to this split: 1 water, 2 firewood, and 2 food. It ensures we can cook, stay hydrated, and keep the campsite clean. Let's finalize this and prepare for the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
